novel_features.name: Library backlog audit
novel_features.description: Surface never-launched games, paid titles you bounced off in under 2 hours, and where your hours actually went by genre.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local SQLite join across owned_games, apps.genres, and apps.is_free that no Steam Web API call provides; every surveyed competitor MCP ships GetOwnedGames raw and stops.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked to plan what to play next, justify a refund, or summarize the user's gaming spend.

novel_features.name: Friend playtime comparison on one app
novel_features.description: Rank everyone in your friend list by hours spent in a specific game, with throttled fan-out so you don't trip Steam's 25 req/s budget.
novel_features.rationale: Steam has no bulk endpoint for 'playtime in app X across N friends'; this combines GetFriendList with per-friend GetOwnedGames behind cliutil.AdaptiveLimiter, then caches to the local owned_games table for subsequent runs.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked 'who in my friends has the most hours in <game>' or 'who owns this and never played'.

novel_features.name: Next easiest achievement to unlock (cross-library)
novel_features.description: Across your entire library, surface the achievement with the highest global unlock percentage that you still don't have.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining player_achievements with achievements_schema.global_pct (which itself merges GetSchemaForGame and GetGlobalAchievementPercentagesForApp at sync time); no surveyed competitor exposes the cross-library 'easiest locked' query.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked 'what should I go unlock next' or to recommend low-effort achievement progress for completionists.

novel_features.name: Per-app achievement workbench
novel_features.description: Render the full achievement schema for one app side-by-side with your unlock state and the global rarity of each achievement in one table.
novel_features.rationale: Joins player_achievements, achievements_schema, and global_pct for one appid; every surveyed MCP exposes the constituent endpoints separately but none ships the joined per-app view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs the full unlock landscape for one game to plan a completion run.

novel_features.name: Rarest unlocked achievements (flex view)
novel_features.description: Surface your rarest achievement unlocks across all owned games — the inverse of the next-achievement query, sorted by ascending global percentage.
novel_features.rationale: Inverse SQL of next-achievement; runs on the same player_achievements x achievements_schema join, no extra data-layer cost.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked to summarize what the user is proud of, or to power a profile-flex panel.

novel_features.name: Library diff between two SteamIDs
novel_features.description: Set operations across two libraries — what's mine-only, what's theirs-only, what's shared with playtime delta.
novel_features.rationale: Pure SQL set ops on owned_games rows for two SteamIDs; no surveyed competitor MCP ships the set-diff primitive even though all of them ship GetOwnedGames.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked 'what games do my friend and I both own' or to plan a co-op session.

novel_features.name: Currently-playing friends
novel_features.description: Show which friends are in-game right now and what they're playing — one batched API call across the friend list, no fanout.
novel_features.rationale: GetPlayerSummaries returns gameextrainfo/gameid in a single batched call across all friend SteamIDs; no surveyed competitor surfaces the joined live-status view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked 'who is online and playing what' or to power a status panel.

novel_features.name: Achievement leaderboard among friends
novel_features.description: Rank your friends by achievement completion percentage for one app, throttled fan-out via the same limiter that powers friends compare.
novel_features.rationale: Throttled GetFriendList x per-friend GetPlayerAchievements(appid) fanout via cliutil.AdaptiveLimiter; persisted to player_achievements so subsequent runs hit the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked 'who in my friends is closest to 100% in <game>' or to seed a competitive completion challenge.

novel_features.name: Review velocity for one app
novel_features.description: Reviews per day and voted-up share over a rolling window for one app — date-bucket aggregation over the cursor-paginated appreviews stream.
novel_features.rationale: Date-bucket SQL on app_reviews.timestamp_created (populated by the appreviews cursor sync); no surveyed competitor MCP ships review-trend aggregation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked to track sentiment shifts after a patch, sale, or controversy.

novel_features.name: News full-text search across tracked apps
novel_features.description: FTS5 search across the title and contents of every news post you've synced, optionally scoped by appid or date range.
novel_features.rationale: SQLite FTS5 over app_news.title and contents; no surveyed competitor MCP ships news search — they ship GetNewsForApp raw and stop.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is asked to dig up patch notes, devlog mentions, or news around a specific topic across the user's library.

novel_features.name: Concurrent-player trend for one app
novel_features.description: Show concurrent-player count over time for one app as a sparkline plus min/max/last over a rolling window — value scales with how often you sample.
novel_features.rationale: Window query over app_player_counts (append-only sampling of GetNumberOfCurrentPlayers); brief explicitly lists this table as time-series append-only. Sparse-output is visible (vs silent) so dogfood-verifiability is preserved.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to see whether a game's playerbase is growing, falling, or has spiked around an event — sample the count every few hours via cron and the window query becomes meaningful within a week.

narrative.headline: Every Steam Web API endpoint, plus a local SQLite store that turns friend playtimes, achievement progress, and library backlogs into single SQL queries no other tool can answer.
narrative.value_prop: Mirrors all 169 documented Steam Web API endpoints (and the undocumented store endpoints every wrapper picks one of) with rate-limit-aware throttling for the post-2025 25 req/s budget. Adds a local SQLite layer with FTS5 over apps, news, and achievements so cross-library queries — `next-achievement`, `friends compare`, `library audit`, `achievement-leaderboard` — run as one command instead of an N+1 fanout app rewrite. Ships an MCP server with both stdio and HTTP streamable transport plus a code-orchestration pair (`steam_web_search` + `steam_web_execute`) so the full surface is reachable without flooding your agent's tool catalog.
narrative.auth_narrative: Standard Steam Web API key auth: get one at https://steamcommunity.com/dev/apikey and set STEAM_WEB_API_KEY in your environment. The key is sent as a `?key=` query parameter on every request. Some endpoints (server time, app list, news) require no auth at all and work without a key. The IAuthenticationService endpoints in the spec are NOT for Web API auth — they implement Steam's interactive QR-code login flow. They remain reachable via the CLI subcommand and the MCP code-orchestration pair for completeness but should not be used as part of normal Web API workflows.

quickstart.comment: Reports whether STEAM_WEB_API_KEY is set in your env. Steam keys are env-var only; export it from your shell rather than running auth set-token.
Probes /ISteamWebAPIUtil/GetServerInfo and a key-gated read to confirm both reachability and your key's validity.
Pulls profile, owned games, friend list, and recent achievements into the local SQLite store; the cross-library novel features all read from this store.
Once sync completes, this is the achievement-hunter's daily ritual — five lowest-effort unlocks across your whole library.
Throttled fan-out across your friend list to rank everyone by hours in app 1245620 (Elden Ring); each call also caches per-friend GetOwnedGames responses to local SQLite for next time.

troubleshoots.symptom: GetOwnedGames returns an empty array even though you own games
troubleshoots.fix: Your Steam profile (or game library) is set to private. Visit https://steamcommunity.com/my/edit/settings, set both 'My profile' and 'Game details' to Public, wait 30s, then retry. Steam returns 200 + empty payload for private profiles instead of a clear error.

Steam tightened rate limits to ~25 req/s in mid-2025. The CLI throttles automatically via cliutil.AdaptiveLimiter, but if you're scripting outside the CLI, slow down. Wait the value of Retry-After (or 60s if absent), then retry.

troubleshoots.symptom: store appdetails returns null for an appid
troubleshoots.fix: Some appids (films, hardware, region-locked) have no store entry. Confirm the appid via `steam-web-pp-cli apps search '<name>'` and try again with the canonical appid.

troubleshoots.symptom: GetUserStatsForGame returns empty for a game you just played
troubleshoots.fix: Steam takes hours-to-days to populate per-user stats for newly-purchased games. Try GetPlayerAchievements instead (achievements populate faster than stats), or wait 24h.

ResolveVanityURL only finds custom URLs (like /id/foo). For numeric profile URLs (/profiles/76561...), the SteamID is already in the URL — no resolve needed. Use `steam-web-pp-cli ISteamUser ResolveVanityURL --vanityurl foo`.

narrative.when_to_use: Pick this CLI when an agent is asked to query Steam profile, library, achievement, or news data and the question crosses entities — anything joining friends with owned games, achievements with global rarity, or news with a date filter. The local SQLite store turns those questions into one SQL query instead of an N+1 fanout. Every other Steam tool is single-shot read-once; this one compounds. For pure single-endpoint reads (one player's profile, one app's news), the per-endpoint MCP tools work too — but the novel commands are where this CLI earns its weight.

recipes.title: Find friends who own a game but never played it
recipes.explanation: Throttled fan-out to identify dormant owners — the people most likely to actually play if invited.

recipes.title: Plan a completion run on one game
recipes.explanation: Surfaces every locked achievement plus its global rarity so you can rank effort vs. reward.

recipes.title: Find low-hanging fruit across your whole library
recipes.explanation: Cross-library SQL — the ten achievements with the highest global unlock rate that you still don't have.

recipes.title: Audit your backlog after a sale
recipes.explanation: Reveals what you bought and didn't open; pairs well with `library audit --bounce` for the under-2h paid set.

recipes.title: Search your synced news feed for a topic
recipes.explanation: FTS5 search over every news post you've synced — useful for tracking when devs ship features you care about across all your owned games.

trigger_phrases: what should I play next on steam
find friends who own this game
who in my friends has the most hours in
